title: Some European Launcher Challenge funding remains in limbo
summary: Nearly €140 million ($162 million) allocated by ESA member states for a European launcher development program remains unallocated and risks being lost. The funding uncertainty could hinder progress in the continent's launch vehicle initiatives.

title: Artemis 2 returns to the pad for April launch attempt
summary: The Artemis 2 launch vehicle and spacecraft have returned to the launch pad for a launch attempt as early as April. This marks a significant step toward NASA's crewed lunar mission.

title: Officina Stellare wins $2 million contract for lasercom ground station in Spain
summary: Officina Stellare secured a $2 million contract to design and build a laser communication ground station in Spain for ICFO. The project will support future space-to-Earth laser and quantum-encrypted communications.

title: Rocket Lab launches eighth Synspective radar imaging satellite
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched the eighth radar imaging satellite for Japanese company Synspective on March 20. This mission marks another step in expanding Synspective's Earth observation capabilities.

title: Another GPS launch shifts from ULA to SpaceX as Vulcan investigation continues
summary: A GPS satellite launch originally planned for ULA's Vulcan Centaur rocket has been reassigned to SpaceX due to ongoing investigations into the Vulcan rocket. The GPS III SV-10 satellite will now fly on a SpaceX launch vehicle.

title: OHB Sweden wins €248 million contract to build EPS-Sterna constellation
summary: OHB Sweden secured a €248 million contract to build 20 satellites for the EPS-Sterna constellation, enhancing Europe's weather forecasting and climate monitoring. This marks the largest contract ever awarded in Sweden’s space sector.

title: Holistic space observation: the shift from SSA to SDA
summary: SpaceX's proposal to deploy up to one million satellites in low Earth orbit, combined with AI-enabled autonomous orbital infrastructure, signals a major shift toward large-scale, strategic space operations. This move reflects a broader industry trend toward holistic space observation and autonomous systems.

title: ESA to fly dedicated Crew Dragon mission to ISS
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will charter a SpaceX Crew Dragon mission to the International Space Station (ISS) to provide additional flight opportunities for its astronauts.

title: Kayhan targets investors, insurers with expanded orbital intelligence platform
summary: Kayhan Space is expanding its orbital intelligence platform to offer business insights for investors and insurers, moving beyond satellite fleet coordination. The new software leverages existing data to cater to financial stakeholders in the space industry.

title: Blue Origin joins the orbital data center race
summary: Blue Origin has entered the orbital data center market by proposing a constellation of up to 51,600 satellites. This move positions the company to compete in the growing space-based data infrastructure sector.

title: Watch Russia launch Progress 94 cargo ship to the ISS on March 22
summary: Russia is set to launch the Progress 94 cargo spacecraft to the International Space Station on March 22. The event will be broadcasted live for public viewing.

title: We chat to legendary space shuttle commander Eileen Collins about her new documentary, 'Spacewoman' (interview)
summary: A new documentary titled 'Spacewoman' highlights the achievements of pioneering astronaut Eileen Collins, a legendary space shuttle commander. The film features an interview with Collins discussing her inspirational career.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 202 — Artemis Imminent
summary: The latest episode of 'This Week In Space' discusses the imminent Artemis missions with Mike Wall, Space.com's spaceflight and tech editor. The podcast covers recent updates and developments related to NASA's Artemis program.

title: Artemis Accords nations grapple with how to handle emergencies and 'harmful interference' on the moon
summary: Nations involved in the Artemis Accords are addressing challenges around emergency responses and preventing harmful interference during lunar missions as moon exploration intensifies. The discussions aim to establish guidelines for managing competing operations on the moon.

title: The loneliest places in the universe might actually be some of the best places for life
summary: Extreme exomoons with tidal heating and hydrogen-rich atmospheres could sustain billions of years of habitable conditions, challenging the idea that isolated places in the universe are lifeless.

title: Would you like to know more about 'Starship Troopers: Ultimate Bug War!'? Developer Auroch Digital has the answers (interview)
summary: Developer Auroch Digital discusses the latest Starship Troopers video game, highlighting its unique twists and strengths compared to previous installments.

title: Astronomers keep finding new moons of Jupiter and Saturn
summary: Astronomers have discovered four new moons orbiting Jupiter and 11 new moons around Saturn, expanding the known celestial bodies in our solar system.

title: US Space Force moves GPS launch to SpaceX Falcon 9 due to Vulcan rocket glitch
summary: The U.S. Space Force has switched an upcoming GPS satellite launch from ULA's Vulcan Centaur to SpaceX's Falcon 9 due to an investigation into a rocket anomaly. The decision highlights ongoing reliability concerns with the Vulcan rocket.

title: Love Project Hail Mary? Here are the Andy Weir books you need to read
summary: The article recommends additional books by Andy Weir after the success of 'Project Hail Mary', highlighting his other works like 'The Martian' and 'Artemis'.

title: 'Project Hail Mary' is an optimistic look towards the stars, and we need that right now
summary: Project Hail Mary offers an optimistic vision of space exploration, emphasizing the need for inspiration in current times. The project highlights the potential of interstellar missions to captivate and motivate.

title: NASA’s X-59 Experimental Supersonic Aircraft Makes Second Flight
summary: NASA's X-59 experimental supersonic aircraft completed its second test flight, marking the start of numerous planned flights in 2026. The abbreviated flight due to a technical issue still provided valuable data before a safe landing.

title: Hangar One Restoration Project
summary: Restoration of Hangar One, a historic aviation landmark in the San Francisco Bay Area, has been completed. The project was finalized by Planetary Ventures in December 2025.

title: NASA Selects University Finalists for Technology Concepts Competition
summary: NASA selected 14 university teams as finalists for the 2026 RASC-AL Competition, challenging students to design innovative concepts for human life and work on the Moon, Mars, and beyond. The competition aims to foster innovation by linking academia with the aerospace community.

title: How Open NASA Data on Comet 3I/ATLAS Will Power Tomorrow’s Discoveries
summary: NASA has collected extensive data on the interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS, which is leaving our solar system, and will make this information publicly available. Over a dozen NASA missions contributed observations of the comet, marking it as the third known interstellar visitor.

title: Smiles and Spacesuits
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams undergoes spacesuit fit verification inside the ISS Quest airlock to ensure airtightness and mobility. The procedure helps mitigate safety risks during spacewalks.

title: NASA Exploration, Science Inspire “Project Hail Mary” Film
summary: NASA's Artemis II crewed mission launch is set to inspire the upcoming film 'Project Hail Mary,' blending real-life space exploration with science fiction.

title: NASA Simulations Improve Artemis II Launch Environment
summary: NASA used advanced simulations to optimize airflow around the Artemis II Space Launch System (SLS) rocket during its test flight to the Moon, ensuring the best possible launch conditions. Engineers employed a NASA-developed tool called the Launch, Ascent, and Vehicle Aerodynamics to study the rocket's flight environment.

title: NASA Glenn Opens Applications for Free Summer Engineering Institute
summary: NASA Glenn Research Center is accepting applications for its free 2026 High School Engineering Institute, a summer program aimed at preparing high school juniors and seniors for careers in aerospace. Applications are open from March 20 to May 1.

title: Restless Kīlauea Launches Lava and Ash
summary: Kīlauea volcano in Hawaii entered its 43rd eruption episode, producing high lava fountains and widespread ash dispersal. This marks another significant event in the ongoing volcanic activity.

title: American Bald Eagle at NASA’s Kennedy Space Center
summary: NASA’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida documented an American bald eagle flying away from its nest during annual nesting surveys. The surveys are conducted to track active and inactive bald eagle nests across Kennedy Space Center, Merritt Island National Wildlife Refuge, and Canaveral National Seashore.

title: Progress MS-33 set to resume Russian flights to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 is scheduled to resume Russian flights to the International Space Station (ISS) from a repaired launch pad. This follows a delay after the Soyuz MS-28 launch from Site 31/6 at Baikonur Cosmodrome.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the production rate of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans for an orbital data center. The company is expanding its manufacturing capabilities and exploring new space-based infrastructure.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the historic Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the crewed lunar flyby mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12 preparations.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: Nine launches are scheduled for the week, including missions by Falcon 9, Spectrum, and Electron rockets. The launches highlight a busy period in the space industry.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin introduced NEO Hunter, a hybrid planetary defense mission concept aimed at protecting Earth from near-Earth objects. The concept combines multiple technologies to detect and mitigate potential asteroid threats.

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its goal of sending humans to the Moon and Mars by transforming steel rolls into Starship components at its Starfactory facility. The company continues to expand its production and innovation efforts in spaceflight.

title: New crew arrivals and science highlight February aboard the ISS
summary: New crew members arrived at the International Space Station (ISS) in February, following the early departure of Crew-11 in January. The month was marked by scientific research and crew activities aboard the ISS.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to dominate another busy week of missions
summary: Eight launches are scheduled worldwide this week, with SpaceX's Falcon 9 dominating the launch schedule. This highlights the rocket's frequent use in current space missions.

title: SpaceX’s Enters a new era with Booster 19 Testing on Pad 2
summary: SpaceX has begun testing Booster 19 on Pad 2, marking a new phase in operations since its last flight 147 days ago. This development signals progress in SpaceX's reusable rocket technology.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on space industry developments without specifying details.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long communication loss due to an anomaly. The mission team confirmed the successful reconnection.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting a series of events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across its programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultra-sensitive cameras, is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical heart. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will release ECOS version 6 on 30 March 2026, mandating its suppliers to use the new costing software for financial proposals in ESA Tender Actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher has developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna for satellite navigation that is resistant to interference and cost-effective compared to bulky alternatives.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Mission teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have initiated experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates microgravity conditions for testing robotic and spacecraft maneuvers. This initiative aims to foster hands-on learning and innovation in space technology among young researchers.

title: Kayhan Space Launches Satcat Terminal
summary: Kayhan Space has launched a new Satcat Terminal business, which may appeal to liberal arts majors. The initiative expands their offerings in the space industry.

title: Inside Austria’s Small But Mighty Space Ecosystem
summary: Austria's space industry is experiencing a renaissance with increased funding and startup activity. The country's small but growing ecosystem is gaining momentum.

title: Portal, Paladin Team On Debris Removal Service
summary: Portal and Paladin are collaborating on a debris removal service to make it operational rather than experimental. The initiative aims to maintain critical satellite infrastructure for communications, navigation, weather forecasting, and national security.

title: HyImpulse Signs Launch Agreement with SaxaVord
summary: HyImpulse has signed a launch agreement with SaxaVord for its SR75 suborbital rocket, with the second flight scheduled for no earlier than Q3 2026 from SaxaVord's launch site.

title: Apex Signs First Japanese Bus Contract With NEC
summary: Apex has signed its first contract in Japan with NEC Corporation to purchase an Aries satellite bus for a communications demonstration mission scheduled for next year.

title: Modified Vulcan Expected to Launch This Summer
summary: The Vulcan rocket has undergone modifications to improve its nozzle and solid rocket booster, with its next launch planned for this summer. The changes were in development before a recent anomaly and are now being prioritized.

title: Advanced Navigation Raises $110M Series C
summary: Advanced Navigation, an Australia-based company, raised $110 million in a Series C funding round to reduce reliance on satellite infrastructure. The investment highlights the growing interest in alternative navigation technologies within the space industry.

title: NanoAvionics Lands Korean National Security Deal
summary: NanoAvionics secured a deal with South Korea's national security sector, marking another success in its global expansion strategy. The agreement highlights NanoAvionics' growing partnerships within the South Korean space industry.

title: FAA Ends Part 450 Transition Period
summary: The FAA has concluded the transition period for Part 450 licensing requirements, with all US launchers now operating under the updated regulations. This marks the end of legacy regulations for commercial space launches.

title: How Congress Can Grow the Space Supply Chain
summary: The space industry's supply chain is struggling to meet rising demand, but policymakers can accelerate growth according to industry groups.

title: Spire Targets 50% Growth in 2026 After Adjustments in 2025
summary: Spire Global aims for a 50% revenue growth in 2026 after divesting its maritime business and reducing debt in 2025. CEO Theresa Condor outlined the ambitious target to investors.

title: Eutelsat CEO Fallacher Talks OneWeb Gen 2, Global Competition, and Sovereign Demand
summary: Eutelsat Group CEO Jean-François Fallacher discusses the company's strategic initiatives, including OneWeb Gen 2, global competition, and sovereign demand, during his nearly one-year tenure. The interview highlights Eutelsat's transformation efforts amid evolving industry dynamics.

title: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space Plan Repeatable Debris Removal Missions
summary: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space are collaborating to provide commercial debris removal missions under a recurring service model called Debris Removal as a Service (DRAAS). This partnership aims to offer repeatable and scalable solutions for space debris mitigation.
company: Portal Space Systems and Paladin Space

title: SLI Purchases Two Small GEO Satellites From ReOrbit
summary: SLI has acquired two small Geostationary Orbit (GEO) communication satellites from ReOrbit for 150 million euros ($172.8 million). The deal was announced in a joint statement by both companies.

title: Comtech Delivers First Set of Digital Modems to US Army
summary: Comtech delivered the first set of Enterprise Digital Intermediate Frequency Multi-Carrier (EDIM) modems to the U.S. Army under a $48.6 million contract signed in 2023. The company announced the delivery on Thursday.

title: Latest White House Cybersecurity Strategy Talks Crypto, AI, Quantum
summary: The White House released President Trump’s Cyber Strategy for America, emphasizing securing critical infrastructure and maintaining leadership in emerging technologies like crypto, AI, and quantum. The strategy outlines six policy pillars to address cybersecurity challenges.

title: Getting Better Outcomes In Space: How Will the UK Embrace the Challenge?
summary: A panel at Space-Comm in London discussed the security of UK space assets and strategic stability from government and industry perspectives.

title: SpiderOak Wins New UAS Systems Contract
summary: SpiderOak has secured a new contract with the U.S. Army Unmanned Aircraft Systems (PMO UAS) to conduct supply chain analysis and cybersecurity assessments for multiple vendors. The contract focuses on enhancing security and reliability in UAS systems.

title: Ukraine Ambassador to UK Talks Future Warfare
summary: The Ukrainian ambassador to the UK discussed the future of warfare at an event in London, four years after Russia's invasion. Valerii Zaluzhnyi highlighted evolving military strategies and challenges.

title: Ligado Tells FCC That SkyTerra Next Proposal Won’t Cause Interference
summary: Ligado has assured the FCC that its SkyTerra Next proposal, involving an L-band payload on AST SpaceMobile satellites, will not cause interference. The company emphasized that the proposal aligns with existing L-band coordination.
